back_translation: The book of the lineage of Jesus Christ, who is (royal) a descendant of David, who is (royal) a descendant of Abraham.
Abraham was the father of Isaac, Isaac was the father of Jacob, Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers,
Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah, with Tamar as mother, Perez was the father of Hezron, Hezron was the father of Aram,
Aram was the father of Amminadab, Amminadab was the father of Nahshon, Nahshon was the father of Salmon,
Salmon was the father of Boaz, with Rahab as mother, Boaz was the father of Obed, with Ruth as mother, Obed was the father of Jesse,
Jesse was the father of David, who is (royal) king. David was the father of Solomon, with the wife of Uriah as mother,
Solomon was the father of Rehoboam, Rehoboam was the father of Abijah, Abijah was the father of Asaph,
Asaph was the father of Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat was the father of Jehoram, Jehoram was the father of Uzziah,
Uzziah was the father of Jotham, Jotham was the father of Ahaz, Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah,
Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh, Manasseh was the father of Amos, Amos was the father of Josiah,
Josiah was the father of Jeconiah and his brothers, at the time of being carried-away to Babylon.
After the being-carried-away to Babylon, Jeconiah was the father of Salathiel, Salathiel was the father of Zerubbabel,
Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud, Abiud was the father of Eliakim, Eliakim was the father of Azor,
Azor was the father of Zadok, Zadok was the father of Achim, Achim was the father of Eliud,
Eliud was the father of Eleazar, Eleazar was the father of Matthan, Matthan was the father of Jacob,
Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary, who was the mother of Jesus who is called Christ.

Therefore, in all, from Abraham to David there were fourteen generations, from David to the being-carried-away to Babylon there were fourteen generations, and from the being-carried-away to Babylon to Christ there were fourteen generations.

The birth (royal) of Jesus Christ was as follows: Mary, his (royal) mother, had been betrothed to Joseph. Before the two of them came to live together, she was found to be pregnant by the Holy Spirit.
Now Joseph, her husband, being a righteous man and not wishing to let her be publicly shamed before people, intended to break off the betrothal with her secretly.

While he was pondering these things, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying: ‹Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because the one conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.
She will give birth to a son, and you shall call his name Jesus, for he will (royal) save his (royal) people from their sins.›
All this happened in order to fulfill what the Lord spoke (royal) through the prophet, saying:
‹Behold, the virgin will have in the womb and will give birth to a son, and they will call his name Immanuel› — which is translated «God is-present (royal) with us».
When Joseph awoke from sleep, he did as the angel of the Lord had commanded, and took Mary as his wife.
But he did not have marital relations with her until she gave birth to a son. Then he called his name Jesus.
